content_markdown:
Two identical cells, each of emf $1.6\text{ V}$ and internal resistance $2.0\text{ }\Omega$, are connected in parallel with a $3.0\text{ }\Omega$ resistor.

What is the current $I$?

diagram_metadata.description: A circuit diagram showing two cells in parallel, each labeled 1.6V, 2.0 Ohms. They are connected to a 3.0 Ohm resistor in a single loop. An ammeter arrow indicates current I flowing through the main branch.

vibe_explanation: When two identical cells are in parallel, the total EMF remains the same as one cell ($1.6\text{ V}$), but the total internal resistance is halved ($2.0\text{ }\Omega / 2 = 1.0\text{ }\Omega$). The total resistance in the circuit is the sum of the equivalent internal resistance and the external resistor ($1.0\text{ }\Omega + 3.0\text{ }\Omega = 4.0\text{ }\Omega$). Using Ohm's law, $I = V/R = 1.6\text{ V} / 4.0\text{ }\Omega = 0.4\text{ A}$.
